next_head_excerpt:
# Chapter 17

Paul watched Tommy through the sliding glass door for a long time before he went out.

Tommy was in the Adirondack chair closest to the railing, Martin's chair whether anybody called it that or not, with a beer balanced on the armrest he was not touching. The propane heater stood dark beside him. The temperature had dropped into the low fifties after sunset, and Tommy was sitting there in a T-shirt and jeans like cold had quit mattering. His hands were on his knees. He was looking at the lake.

Joel was in the study with the RT envelope open on Martin's desk and a yellow legal pad filling up in his square public-defender block print. He had been at it since they read the document and pulled Cliff's phone extraction. Fourteen photos of Martin's pages. Texts to a Minneapolis lawyer about leverage. Times. Dates. A clean line from a dead man opening a safe on Sunday morning to another dead man going under at the dock on Monday night.

Joel wanted to walk it straight into the kitchen and wait for Mallick to come back from Detroit Lakes.

"We hand her this tonight," he had said.

"I know."

"Then let's stop pretending there's a reason not to."

Paul had been standing by the lake print, the one that hid the safe, still holding the frame because he had not bothered rehanging it yet. His fingers were on the wire backing. He could feel dust from the wall in the creases of his skin.

"Give me an hour," he said.

Joel looked at him over the top of the legal pad. "For what."

"To talk to him."

"About Cliff?"

"About all of it."

"Paul, this isn't an exit interview. This i...
open_hooks_to_carry: Joel and Paul on the deck comparing notes — Paul's suspicion about Martin-Tommy, Joel's observations about Tommy-Cliff
They go to the study and find the fire document in the safe — the envelope has been unfolded twice by different hands
Joel reads Martin's account of the 2002 fire and Ray Toomey's death
The picture assembles: Cliff found the document, Cliff leveraged Tommy, Tommy killed Cliff
Joel and Paul must decide: go to Mallick, confront Tommy, or protect the group one last time the way Martin would have
Joel's legal mind and Paul's loyalty to Martin's memory should pull in opposite directions

payoffs_to_land.description: Joel and Paul find and read the fire document that Cliff discovered in Ch 8 and used as leverage in Ch 12. The crease pattern reveals it was handled by multiple people. The full picture of the murder motive is now assembled.

prev_tail_excerpt:
Tommy leaned forward in the chair. "You don't have shit."

"Not yet," Mallick said. "But I have your lie, I have your meeting, and I have a dead man with the wrong shoes and no phone. That's enough to keep me interested."

He stood so fast the chair legs barked against the floor.

Arneson took one step into the room. "Sit down."

Tommy looked at him, then at her. For a second the coaching voice threatened to come back. She could see it building, the instinct to get louder, bigger, more certain than the room. It died before it reached his mouth.

He sat.

Mallick closed the notebook.

"Here's what happens next," she said. "You stay where a deputy can see you. You don't touch your room, your boots, or a sink. Mr. Griggs and I go through every file on that phone until daylight if we have to. And before this night is over, somebody is going to tell me what Haywood had that made him think he could call you to the dock like that."

Tommy's face changed at the last part. It wasn't enough for court. It was plenty for her.

She stood and opened the study door.

"Arneson. Sit on him."

"Yep."

"Kelleher, bring Griggs upstairs in five minutes. I want Cliff Haywood's phone found before sunrise. Search the yard, the path, the shoreline, the trash, the couch cushions, the whole damn house."

"Copy."

Outside, down by the trees, another flashlight came on and started moving toward the dock.
secondary_character_beats: Joel's public defender instincts should make him aware of what this evidence means legally — and what it doesn't prove without confession
state_deltas_required: Joel and Paul assembling the full picture from civilian evidence
Joel and Paul know Tommy killed Cliff — the question is what they do with it
